title: Dara and the Shy Little Hamster
synopsis: When Dara discovers her classroom's new hamster hiding and looking scared, she uses kindness and patience to help the little animal feel brave and happy in its new home.

pages.text: Dara bounced into her classroom with a big smile. She loved school! Her teacher, Ms. Rodriguez, had curly black hair and kind brown eyes. "Children, we have a special surprise today," Ms. Rodriguez said. "Meet our new class pet!"

illustration.description: A bright classroom with colorful alphabet posters on yellow walls. Dara, a young Black girl with two neat braids tied with purple ribbons, wears a cheerful orange dress and has an excited expression. Ms. Rodriguez, a Latina teacher with shoulder-length curly black hair, stands near a small cage covered with a blue cloth. Other diverse children sit on a rainbow-colored carpet, all looking curious and happy.
illustration.alt: Dara and her diverse classmates sitting excitedly in their bright classroom as their teacher reveals a surprise

pages.text: Ms. Rodriguez pulled away the blue cloth. Inside the cage was a tiny brown and white hamster. But something was wrong. The little hamster was hiding in the corner, looking very scared. His tiny body was shaking, and his dark eyes looked worried.

illustration.description: A close-up view of a small glass cage with wood shavings on the bottom. A small brown and white hamster with big dark eyes huddles in the far corner behind a tiny red water bottle, looking frightened with his ears back. The cage has a yellow food bowl and an orange exercise wheel. Dara's concerned face is visible at the edge of the frame, her brown eyes wide with empathy.
illustration.alt: A scared little hamster hiding in the corner of his cage while Dara looks on with concern

pages.text: "Why is he so scared?" asked Dara. She felt sad seeing the hamster hide. Ms. Rodriguez explained gently, "This is Peanut's first day here. New places can feel scary sometimes. He needs time to feel safe and happy." Dara nodded. She remembered feeling nervous on her first day of school too.

illustration.description: Dara kneels beside the cage with a thoughtful, caring expression on her face. Her hand is pressed gently against the glass. Ms. Rodriguez crouches next to her, one hand on Dara's shoulder, speaking softly. Other children including a boy with glasses and a girl with blonde pigtails gather around, all looking concerned. The classroom has warm lighting and the hamster is still visible hiding in his corner.
illustration.alt: Dara and Ms. Rodriguez having a gentle conversation about the scared hamster while other children listen

pages.text: Dara had an idea. "Can I sit quietly by Peanut?" she asked. Ms. Rodriguez smiled and said yes. Dara sat very still next to the cage. She spoke in a soft, gentle voice. "Hi, Peanut. I'm Dara. You don't have to be scared. We're all friends here."

illustration.description: Dara sits cross-legged on the floor beside Peanut's cage, speaking softly with a warm, encouraging smile. She's leaning slightly toward the cage but giving the hamster space. Her posture is calm and patient. The hamster has moved slightly closer, peeking out from behind his water bottle with one curious eye visible. Soft morning sunlight streams through a window with paper cutout flowers taped to it.
illustration.alt: Dara sitting patiently beside the hamster cage, speaking gently to help Peanut feel safe

pages.text: Slowly, very slowly, Peanut crept forward. Dara stayed perfectly still and kept talking softly. "You're being so brave, Peanut," she whispered. The little hamster's nose twitched. He took another tiny step forward. Dara felt happy and excited, but she didn't move. She wanted Peanut to feel safe.

illustration.description: The hamster has moved to the middle of his cage, standing on his hind legs with his tiny pink nose pressed against the glass. His posture shows curiosity rather than fear now. Dara remains motionless, her eyes bright with joy and her smile gentle and encouraging. Her classmates watch from a respectful distance in the background, all with pleased expressions. The scene has a warm, golden glow suggesting success and friendship.
illustration.alt: Peanut the hamster approaching the front of his cage while Dara watches with quiet joy

pages.text: Finally, Peanut came all the way to the front of his cage! He looked right at Dara with bright, brave eyes. He wasn't scared anymore - he looked happy! "You did it, Peanut!" Dara whispered. She felt proud and joyful. Ms. Rodriguez smiled. "Thank you for being so patient and kind, Dara. You helped Peanut feel welcome."

illustration.description: Peanut stands confidently at the front of his cage with his tiny paws against the glass, looking directly at Dara with bright, alert eyes and a content expression. Dara beams with pride and happiness, her face glowing with joy. Ms. Rodriguez stands behind them with an approving smile. The other children clap quietly in the background. The whole scene is filled with warm, celebratory colors and everyone looks delighted with the success.
illustration.alt: A happy ending with Peanut feeling brave and confident while Dara celebrates helping him feel welcome

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Peanut the hamster feel at first?
questions.explanation: Peanut was hiding in the corner and shaking because he was scared in his new place.

questions.question: What did Dara do to help Peanut?
choices: She sat quietly and spoke softly
questions.explanation: Dara sat very still and spoke in a gentle voice to help Peanut feel safe.

questions.question: How did Dara feel when Peanut came to the front of his cage?
questions.explanation: Dara felt happy and proud because she helped Peanut feel brave and welcome.

questions.question: How did Peanut feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: By the end, Peanut wasn't scared anymore - he felt brave and happy in his new home.

pages.text: Dara felt _____ seeing the hamster hide in the corner.
Peanut was _____ on his first day at school.
Dara spoke in a _____ voice to help Peanut feel safe.
